source.description: Built-in image generation created a six-panel marker process contact sheet for the goofy-monster-face lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet was reviewed before cropping; the final panel is used as the finished asset.

process_strategy: The sequence keeps one centered monster head, introducing the head, horns, eyes, mouth, teeth, spots, outline, and marker color before the final punch-up.

final_elements.name: round monster head
final_elements.description: A round blobby head gives the monster a friendly silhouette.

final_elements.name: two little horns
final_elements.description: Two short rounded horns sit on top of the head.

final_elements.name: uneven eyes
final_elements.description: One large eye and one smaller eye make the expression goofy.

final_elements.name: wide toothy mouth
final_elements.description: A wide smile with simple square teeth crosses the lower face.

final_elements.name: cheek spots
final_elements.description: A few round cheek spots add playful monster texture.

final_elements.name: bold marker color
final_elements.description: Green, purple, and yellow marker fills make the face bright and doodly.

frames.visible_job: Draw the large rounded blobby head outline with a slightly flat lower edge.
Place two little rounded horns on the top of the head.
Add one large eye and one smaller eye to create the goofy expression.
Draw a wide mouth and add simple square teeth along the smile.
Add cheek spots, thicken the black outline, and fill the monster with green, purple, and yellow marker.

final_step.visible_job: Strengthen the existing black outline, deepen the marker fills, and add tiny highlights to already-drawn eyes and teeth.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
